    "Look-through sector concentration is meaningful: Financials (HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Kotak Bank, Bajaj Finance) at approximately 34 percent of strategy weight, FMCG (ITC, HUL, Britannia, Marico, Asian Paints, Pidilite) at approximately 41 percent; top-2 sector weight aggregates to 75 percent.",
    "FMCG sector cycle positioning is mid-cycle with margin pressure: rural volume softness persisting through FY26, premiumisation thesis intact but slower in delivery; Nifty FMCG trailing 12-month earnings growth 6.8 percent against 10-year average 11.4 percent.",

  "reasoning_paragraph": "The Marcellus book is sector-coherent by design: quality screens push it toward Financials and FMCG, both sectors where consistent compounding is structurally available. The same coherence creates a concentration: the household, layered on top of its existing direct large-cap and active MF positions, will deepen exposure to those two sectors. FMCG is the more pressing concern of the two; rural volume softness has persisted across the FY26 print cycle and the strategy's premium valuation assumes a return to mid-teens earnings growth that has not yet materialised at the category level. Financials look reasonable on cycle but pricing pressure on private bank deposit cost is a watchpoint. The moat assessment is the cleanest part of the read: the underlying businesses are genuinely high-quality and competitive structure is favourable.",
